issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the hamburger trigger. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1530: function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 1550: function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may not announce the element as a toggleable control until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: _initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is shown and hidden via CSS classes but its aria-hidden state is never updated. The drawer content may remain reachable by screen readers even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1530: addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
Line 1550: removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update CSS classes and scroll locks but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the hamburger trigger. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1419: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
Line 1441: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid="true" state on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1297: `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not reset the aria-invalid state to "false". The field may continue to be announced as invalid even after the user has corrected the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1306: `field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby'); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization function does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger. Screen readers may not identify the button as a toggle control until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger is not programmatically linked to the navigation drawer it controls. Screen reader users cannot easily jump from the trigger to the newly revealed content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.fix: Line 2273: `applyFilters(); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(value));`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. Users who rely on keyboard navigation will be unable to track focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes the focus ring for all button elements, making keyboard navigation unusable for users who rely on visual focus cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This does not hide content visually and will expose it to sighted users, while the intended screen-reader-only content will be visible and layout-breaking.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

Users with colour blindness or low vision may not be able to identify links.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button (role="switch") does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked when toggled, leaving screen-reader users unaware of its state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, set aria-pressed on the toggle button when the period changes. Example: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated card count and visible results are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge and empty state, call liveRegion.announce with a message such as "Showing X products".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load, so screen-reader users do not know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element (_trigger).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialised on page load, so screen-reader users do not know the initial state of the dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, set aria-expanded="false" on each trigger element.

issuesFound.title: Billing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the update.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the prices, call liveRegion.announce with a message such as "Prices switched to annual billing".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav open function, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in the close function, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); and in the toggle function, update accordingly.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); in _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons (monthly/annual) change the billing period but do not set aria-pressed to indicate which button is selected. Screen reader users cannot perceive which option is active.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive); for each period button.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce dynamic content updates
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid but does not provide an aria-live announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the grid, call liveRegion.announce with a message such as "Showing X products".

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce dynamic content updates
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates displayed prices but does not provide an aria-live announcement. Screen reader users are not notified of the billing period change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce with a message such as "Prices switched to annual billing".

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.goTo does not announce slide changes
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.goTo function changes the visible slide but does not provide an aria-live announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when the carousel advances.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the slide, call liveRegion.announce with a message such as "Slide X of Y".

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be alerted to validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); when valid, set it to false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads and the drawer is hidden. Screen reader users may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init function, set setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); if the drawer is hidden on load.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads and panels are hidden. Screen reader users may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu register function, set setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); for each trigger.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set to reflect the initial open/closed state on page load. Screen reader users may not know which items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In the faqAccordion init function, set setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, item.open); for each FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria-hidden on the drawer

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not manage focus when opening/closing

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not manage focus when opening/closing

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle) opens the navigation drawer (SELECTORS.navDrawer) but never receives an aria‑controls reference to the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state is never toggled, leaving users unaware of the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑period toggle switch missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing toggle (data‑billing‑toggle) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the annual plan is active.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not toggle aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open() adds the suggestion list but never sets aria‑expanded on the search input, breaking the disclosure relationship.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds error messages but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the associated input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form does not reflect validation errors via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: newsletterForm._onSubmit() validates the email field but never sets aria‑invalid when the field is invalid, leaving the error invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Contact form does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: contactForm._onSubmit() uses inlineValidator.validate() but never adds aria‑invalid to the offending inputs, preventing AT users from detecting errors.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not set aria‑controls on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never aria‑controls pointing to the panel’s id, breaking the explicit relationship.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not set aria‑controls on the input (already set) but missing aria‑expanded handled in Issue 5 – also missing aria‑activedescendant cleanup on close
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._close() removes aria‑activedescendant only when an active index exists; if the list is closed without a selection the attribute may linger, confusing AT.

fpTitles: Newsletter form does not reflect validation errors via aria‑invalid
Contact form does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set `aria-hidden` on the drawer when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the element remains in the accessibility tree (no `aria-hidden` or `hidden` attribute), causing screen readers to encounter hidden navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` link to drawer ID
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button controls the drawer but never receives an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `nav-drawer`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial `aria-expanded="false"` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: After registration the trigger receives `aria-haspopup` but never receives an explicit `aria-expanded` state, leaving its default undefined.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count changes but no `aria-live` region is updated, so users of screen readers are unaware of the new total.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` (e.g., `#product-count-status`) and update its text with the new count each time `_updateCountBadge` runs.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria-invalid` on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: Validation adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, preventing AT from conveying the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new price to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggles, price numbers change visually but no live‑region is used to announce the new amounts, leaving AT users uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each price element, also update a hidden `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` (e.g., `#price-announcement`) with a concise message like “Monthly price now $X”.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button does not expose `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles automatic rotation but never reflects its state via `aria-pressed`, making it impossible for AT users to know whether autoplay is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `togglePlay`, after changing `_playing`, call `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks `aria-hidden` when opened (exposes hidden content)
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer opens, it becomes visible but the code never removes `aria-hidden` (if it was present) nor ensures it is absent, potentially leaving a stale `aria-hidden="true"` that blocks AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()`, add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`; in `close()`, set `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hide animation does not set `aria-hidden` on the panel while hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via CSS classes and `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is never applied, so AT may still read its contents.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance`, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_openInstance`, remove that attribute.  

These ten issues satisfy all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and reflect concrete line locations from the source file.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu hide animation does not set `aria-hidden` on the panel while hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but they never set or clear `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Assistive technologies cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close()` (or use `toggleAttr`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls linking button to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the drawer element’s id, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds a CSS class, but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not clear aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, so the state remains ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance()`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not toggle aria‑expanded when suggestion list opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The search component shows/hides the suggestion list via `suggestionListRenderer.show()/hide()`, but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>` element, violating the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: When opening (`_open()`), call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; when closing, set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list renderer does not manage aria‑expanded on the combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount()` function creates the listbox and sets `aria‑autocomplete`, `aria‑controls`, etc., but does not set `aria‑expanded` on the associated input.
issuesFound.fix: In `mount()`, after wiring attributes, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`; then update it to `'true'`/`'false'` in the open/close logic of the search component.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks required aria‑checked for a switch role
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `[data-billing-toggle]` is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑checked` attribute, so its on/off state is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: When initializing, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false')`; update to `'true'` when the annual period is active and back to `'false'` for monthly.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not expose aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons with `data-period` are used as a radio‑style group, but the code only toggles a CSS class (`CSS.active`). No `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑selected`) attribute is updated, leaving the selected period undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState()`, after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each button.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In `open(item)`, the function adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on `item.trigger`. The close function does set it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of `open(item)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not remove aria‑hidden from the panel; close does not set it
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s visibility is controlled via the `hidden` attribute, but the ARIA attribute `aria‑hidden` is never cleared on open nor set on close, so screen readers may still treat the panel as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)`, after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')`, also `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`. In `close(item)`, after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack aria‑controls linking to step panels
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s “Next” and “Prev” buttons control which step panel is shown, but they do not have `aria-controls` referencing the associated panel ids, nor do the panels have `aria-labelledby` linking back to the buttons. This hampers assistive navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each step panel an id (`srUtils.ensureId(step.panelEl, 'wizard-step')`) and set `setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', step.panelEl.id)` / `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', step.panelEl.id)`. Also set `setAttr(step.panelEl, 'aria-labelledby', _nextBtn.id)` as appropriate.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack aria‑expanded or live announcements for slide changes
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel updates slides visually but does not announce the new slide via an ARIA live region or update `aria‑expanded`/`aria‑selected` on the dot navigation (only CSS class is toggled). While dots get `aria-selected`, the main carousel container does not have a live region to announce slide changes for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After `goTo(index)`, call `liveRegion.announce` (already done) but also ensure the carousel container has `role="region"` and `aria-live="polite"` (add in markup or via JS).  

These issues cover the mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and highlight missing ARIA state management in toggle components, comboboxes, switches, and accordions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav state changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-brand address uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on background var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: .footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; } (where --colour-neutral-300 is #cbd5e1, providing ~5.9:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (minimum 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-bottom > p uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on background #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: .social-links a uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.0:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) for the default link state, causing links to rely solely on colour distinction from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, leaving keyboard users with no visible focus indicator. The outline-offset property has no effect when outline width is zero, so no focus ring is rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule removes the outline and provides only an outline-offset, leaving high-contrast mode users without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 395-398: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, rendering the utility non-functional.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410-427: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition including transform properties and an active state transform translation, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 571: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 493) Line 493-494: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images have a transform transition and hover transform effect without a prefers-reduced-motion override, potentially causing issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 927-931: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Statistics items have a transition including transform and a hover transform effect, but no reduced-motion override is provided for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: (add new rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Hero call-to-action buttons have a transition including transform and a hover transform translation without a reduced-motion override, potentially affecting users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 877: (add new rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: Featured pricing cards have a hover transform effect that is not covered by the existing .pricing-card reduced-motion override, leaving motion animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1217: (add to existing @media at line 1183) Line 1183-1185: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing set to -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .how-it-works > p

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not set aria-expanded when showing or hiding the dropdown panel, leaving screen reader users unaware of the submenu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2332: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 2347: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not toggle aria-expanded when opening or closing the suggestion list, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3152: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 3167: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`
  Line 3225: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle missing aria-expanded

  Line 4150: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) function as toggle buttons but do not set aria-pressed to indicate the active selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3582: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element has role="switch" but never sets aria-checked to reflect whether the annual option is selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3625: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`
  Line 3545: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(period === 'year'));`
  Line 3585: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation utility functions update error descriptions but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields, preventing screen readers from announcing validation state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1872: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
  Line 1892: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Grid/List view toggle buttons initialize without aria-pressed, so screen reader users don't know which view is active on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3882: Add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button initializes without aria-pressed when autoplay is disabled, failing to communicate the non-playing state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4422: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change missing live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price text visually but provides no announcement to screen reader users about the change or new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3545: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge without announcing the results to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3770: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content without announcing the action or current state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4125: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));`
  Line 4150: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));`

  Line 2192: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion missing aria-selected update
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard navigation through search suggestions updates the active item visually but fails to set aria-selected="true" on the active option and "false" on others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3075: Replace the existing forEach block with: `items.forEach((item, i) => { const isActive = i === index; toggleClass(item, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(item, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: Focus traps used for modal dialogs and drawers do not set aria-modal="true" on the container element, causing screen readers to potentially navigate outside the modal content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1992: Add `setAttr(container, ARIA.MODAL, 'true');`

fpTitles: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
mobileNav missing live announcement
siteSearch suggestion missing aria-selected update

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1880: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 1900: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function adds the open CSS class and animates the panel height, but unlike the close function which sets aria-expanded="false", it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the button in an ambiguous state for screen readers when the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5155: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on page load. Additionally, the _open and _close functions toggle the listbox visibility without updating aria-expanded to reflect the open or closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3080: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the init function after setting aria-haspopup.
  Line 3130: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in the _open function after setting _searchState.open = true.
  Line 3145: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the _close function after setting _searchState.open = false.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on the invalid field. Conversely, srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1290: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function.
  Line 1305: Insert `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function in productFilter updates the product grid visibility and count badges after filtering, but it does not announce the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3680: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge is called.

issuesFound.title: pricing module does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function in the pricing module updates price displays and discount badges when users switch between monthly and annual billing, but it does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have updated or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4385: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing\`);` after the forEach loop that updates plan cards in the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module updates the active CSS class on billing period buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which button is currently selected. This prevents screen readers from identifying the active billing period in the tab-like interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4420: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open CSS class from the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. While CSS may visually hide the drawer, it remains in the accessibility tree. Conversely, when opened, aria-hidden should be removed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1745: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` in the close function after removing the open class.
  Line 1725: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` in the open function after adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-controls on the trigger button and initializes the panel with the hidden attribute, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger during setup. This leaves the initial expanded state ambiguous for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the register function after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function discovers the trigger and drawer elements and wires up event listeners, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button during setup. This violates the requirement that toggle buttons must indicate their initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1785: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the init function after the _initialised = true assignment.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers open and close panels via `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`, but neither function sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger element to indicate the dropdown's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2903: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside `_openInstance`. Line 2913: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: While the `close` function sets `aria-expanded="false"`, the `open` function never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3708: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button does not update aria-checked (or aria-pressed)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) has `role="switch"` but `_syncToggleState` only toggles CSS classes. It never updates `aria-checked` (or `aria-pressed`) to indicate whether the switch is in the "year" (checked) or "month" (unchecked) state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3498: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear);` inside `_syncToggleState` (or use `ARIA.PRESSED` if keeping role button).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"`, so screen readers do not announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1206: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside `markInvalid`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared/validated, the function removes the error description but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` (or remove the attribute), leaving the field incorrectly flagged as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1228: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`) at the end of `markValid`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set, violating the requirement that toggle buttons indicate their initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2862: After confirming `_trigger` exists, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is not programmatically associated with the drawer it controls via `aria-controls`, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2863: After ensuring `_drawer` has an id (using `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')`), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: While `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` are set, `aria-expanded` is not initialized to `"false"` on the trigger when the dropdown is registered in the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2968: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS class removal, but the code does not set `aria-hidden="true"` (or the `hidden` attribute) on the drawer element itself. If CSS uses visibility or off-screen positioning rather than `display: none`, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2820: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` inside `close`. Correspondingly, add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` in the `open` function (line 2810).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the navigation drawer is a significant context change that is not announced to screen reader users via the `liveRegion` utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2811: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` in `open`. Line 2827: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in `close`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced, leaving screen reader users unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');` in `_openInstance`. Line 2916: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items is not announced to screen readers, even though visual content changes occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3725: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` in `open`. Line 3744: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` in `close`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes and cards animate, but no announcement is made via `liveRegion` to inform screen reader users of the result count or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3093: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually, but the change is not announced to screen reader users, who may miss the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3492: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` (or similar string indicating the switch).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares `_trap` and references it in `close`, but never initializes or activates a focus trap in the `open` function. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2812: After opening the drawer, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers does not set aria-current on last item
issuesFound.explanation: The comment indicates the last item should be marked as current page, but the implementation is missing. The last link lacks `aria-current="page"`, so screen readers cannot identify the current page in the breadcrumb.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion-style submenu trigger in MobileNav toggles the isExpanded state but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing if the submenu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger receives isOpen prop but does not set aria-expanded to communicate the disclosure state to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid. When the input has a validation error, screen reader users are not notified of the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, failing to communicate error states to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing screen readers from announcing error states.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in DesktopNav that represent the current page (item.current === true) receive a visual modifier class but lack aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is active.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in MobileNav that represent the current page receive visual styling but lack aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from identifying the active page.

      className="testimonials-track__prev"

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons manage a selected state (value === 'monthly' or 'annual') but lack aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from knowing which billing period is selected.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not set aria-busy="true" when loading, so screen reader users are not notified that the button is processing and the action is in progress.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" separately from the "required" text that follows, creating redundant and confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~720: Add aria-hidden to the marker span: {required && ( <> * (required) )}

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an article element containing a heading with a generated ID, but the article lacks aria-labelledby referencing that heading, preventing screen reader users from identifying the card's accessible name when navigating by landmarks or articles.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1580: Add aria-labelledby to the article:

{plan.name}

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a visually hidden h2 with id="stats-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, so the section landmark is not properly labelled for screen reader navigation.

Luminary Studio by the numbers

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, leaving the landmark unlabelled for screen reader users navigating by regions.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish this navigation landmark from other navigation regions on the page.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing aria-label

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component applies an aria-label to the container but the individual star characters (★/☆) are exposed to assistive technology as separate text nodes, causing redundant and confusing announcements alongside the container's label.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The IntegrationsBar renders a duplicate list of integrations for visual marquee looping purposes, but this clone is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen reader users to hear the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not visible (isVisible === false) are only hidden via CSS classes but remain accessible to screen readers via the DOM, causing users to encounter content that is not visually displayed.

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component displays formatted values like "40,000+" but does not use the valueLabel from the Stat interface (e.g., "40,000 plus") as an aria-label, so screen readers may not announce the value correctly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1220: Add aria-label using stat.valueLabel:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog". This incorrectly marks the backdrop as modal instead of the dialog itself, potentially causing assistive technology to behave unexpectedly.
